prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Call of Cthulhu / 1920s Lovecraftian inspired cosmic horror atlas for the terrain type dual_continent.
Scene/backdrop: two major 1920s coastal continents separated by uneven cold seas and narrow straits, with New England-style coastlines, Arkham-like university towns, Miskatonic river deltas, fog-shrouded rocky hills, old colonial settlements, lighthouse coasts, cult-haunted villages, forbidden standing stones, old rail lines, marshlands and strange ruins.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated 1920s investigative atlas, aged parchment with hand annotations, sepia ink, damp fog, creeping cosmic dread, subtle impossible geometry and occult diagram motifs.
Composition/framing: tall portrait map, asymmetric continents of different sizes; the sea separation is diagonal and S-shaped with C-shaped gulfs, L-shaped channels and jagged straits, not a simple vertical divider; include compass rose, hand-drawn border, ship/rail routes, lighthouse icons, standing stone symbols and investigator annotations without readable text.
Geography constraints: realistic complex coastlines with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, capes, broken shores, curved coasts, deltas, salt marshes, foggy estuaries, inland seas, cliff coasts and varied landforms; span rocky coasts, dark forests, rural farmlands, mountains, wetlands, cold islands, moors and uncanny wasteland regions.
Avoid: heroic fantasy, visible tentacle monster centerpiece, mirrored continents, modern satellite image, clean tourist map, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
